skill.name: inngest
skill.description: Inngest expert for serverless-first background jobs, event-driven workflows, and durable execution without managing queues or workers. Use when: inngest, serverless background job, event-driven workflow, step function, durable execution.
skill.summary: Design durable Inngest workflows for serverless background jobs, scheduled tasks, and event-driven product automation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against the only scanned file. The weak-cryptography and shell-backtick alerts are false positives from prose and Markdown code spans, with no executable behavior found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alert
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged SKILL.md line 3, but the line is only the skill description. No cryptographic algorithm or crypto API usage appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains plain frontmatter describing Inngest capabilities. I found no weak cryptographic algorithm or related implementation evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged six backtick patterns on SKILL.md line 55. The line only lists related skill names in Markdown code spans.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown formatting around related skill labels, not shell execution. There is no Ruby, command string, or tool invocation.

content.value_statement: Serverless apps still need reliable background work and long-running processes. This skill helps design Inngest functions, steps, schedules, and event workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains Inngest function setup for typed events in Next.js.
Guides multi-step workflows with checkpoints and error handling.
Covers scheduled and cron-based Inngest functions.
Advises on fan-out patterns and concurrency control.
Identifies common anti-patterns such as large payloads and missing steps.
limitations: Does not include runnable source files or reusable code templates.
Does not manage Inngest accounts, deployments, or production infrastructure.
Does not verify live API behavior or current Inngest pricing.
Provides guidance, not a complete application implementation.

use_cases.title: Add Background Jobs to a Web App
use_cases.description: Plan event-driven jobs for email, billing, notifications, or data synchronization without managing workers.

use_cases.title: Design Long-Running Product Workflows
use_cases.description: Break onboarding, fulfillment, or AI generation flows into durable steps that can retry after failures.

use_cases.title: Review Inngest Architecture Choices
use_cases.description: Evaluate event payloads, concurrency settings, schedules, and workflow boundaries before production release.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic Function
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new background task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Inngest function for this background task: [describe task]. Include event name, payload fields, steps, and retry behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Queue Job
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this queue worker into an Inngest workflow: [describe worker]. Show events, step boundaries, idempotency concerns, and failure handling.

prompt_templates.title: Review Workflow Reliability
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Inngest workflow design for reliability: [describe workflow]. Check concurrency, retries, payload size, fan-out, and observability.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Durable System
prompt_templates.prompt: Architect a durable Inngest system for [business process]. Include events, scheduled functions, long sleeps, compensation steps, and operational risks.

output_examples.input: Plan a welcome email flow after a user signs up.
output_examples.output: A recommended event name, compact payload, separate email and analytics steps, retry guidance, and a delayed follow-up step.

output_examples.input: Review an AI generation workflow that can take several minutes.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step reliability review covering checkpoints, retries, fan-out limits, progress updates, and payload reduction.
A scheduled function outline with batching, concurrency limits, idempotency checks, and failure recovery notes.

best_practices: Keep event payloads small and fetch large records inside steps.
Use explicit steps for every side effect that needs retry safety.
Define concurrency limits before workflows reach production traffic.
anti_patterns: Putting all workflow logic inside one uncheckpointed function body.
Sending huge serialized objects through events instead of stable identifiers.
Ignoring retries, idempotency, and duplicate event delivery.

faq.question: What is this skill best for?
faq.answer: It is best for designing serverless background jobs and durable workflows with Inngest.

faq.question: Does it generate complete production code?
faq.answer: It provides architecture and implementation guidance, but the skill file does not include complete code templates.

faq.question: Can it help with Next.js apps?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explicitly references Inngest function setup with typed events in Next.js.

faq.question: Does it replace Inngest documentation?
faq.answer: No. Use official documentation for current APIs, limits, and deployment details.

faq.question: Can it review existing workflow designs?
faq.answer: Yes. It can assess step boundaries, concurrency, payload size, schedules, and retry behavior.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains guidance text only, and the static alerts were false positives.
